name: odbc
label: ODBC
description: ODBC (Open Database Connectivity) allows applications to access data from different database systems (e.g., MySQL, SQL Server, Oracle). It enables interoperability between applications and databases through an ODBC driver.

fields.title: Connection Method
options: Connect using a DSN (Data Source Name)
fields.name: connection_type
fields.description: Select the method to establish the ODBC connection. You can either use a pre-configured DSN (Connect using a DSN) on the system or or provide a full DSN-less connection string (Connect using a DSN-less connection).

Connect using a DSN (Data Source Name).title: Data Source Name (DSN)
Connect using a DSN (Data Source Name).name: dsn_name
Connect using a DSN (Data Source Name).description: Specify the Data Source Name to establish the ODBC connection.
Connect using a DSN (Data Source Name).tooltip: Specify the Data Source Name to establish the ODBC connection.

Connect using a DSN-less connection.title: Username
Connect using a DSN-less connection.name: username
Connect using a DSN-less connection.tooltip: Specify the username for ODBC authentication. Note: For Azure Databricks, this should be 'token'.
Connect using a DSN-less connection.description: Specify the username for ODBC authentication. Note: For Azure Databricks, this should be 'token'.

Connect using a DSN-less connection.title: Password
Connect using a DSN-less connection.name: token
Connect using a DSN-less connection.tooltip: Specify the personal access token (PAT) used for ODBC authentication.
Connect using a DSN-less connection.description: Specify the personal access token (PAT) used for ODBC authentication.

Connect using a DSN-less connection.title: Connection String
Connect using a DSN-less connection.name: connection_str
Connect using a DSN-less connection.description:
Specify the connection string to use to connect to database. 
For example, Driver={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};Host=myserver.database.windows.net;Port=1433;UID=myuser;PWD=mypassword;SSL=1;Database=mydatabase;Encrypt=yes;TrustServerCertificate=no;
Connect using a DSN-less connection.tooltip:
Specify the connection string to use to connect to database. 
For example, Driver={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};Host=myserver.database.windows.net;Port=1433;UID=myuser;PWD=mypassword;SSL=1;Database=mydatabase;Encrypt=yes;TrustServerCertificate=no;

operations.title: Execute Query
operations.description: Executes a custom SQL query on the connected ODBC data source and returns the query results.

parameters.title: Query
parameters.tooltip: Specify the SQL query to execute on the target ODBC database.
parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Specify the SQL query to execute on the target ODBC database.
